title: The Read vs. The Touch
concept: The level did not hold or break; the flow at the level held or broke, and the flow was on the screen the whole time.
teaching_objective: Convert a level touch into a conditional, evidence-based read before the outcome resolves, instead of treating the touch as an entry trigger.
junior_error: The touch-as-signal reflex: shorting 5484.00 because price hit resistance, with no response signature required first.
professional_read: The touch raises information density; the read is built from how the auction responds: volume signature, delta versus displacement, DOM refresh versus pull, speed change, and post-touch structure.
decision_consequence: Absorption with no upward progression resolves to a responsive short; a lift-through that holds the retest resolves to a continuation long. Same line, opposite decisions.

forbidden_errors.description: Do not imply the touch itself is a tradable signal.
Do not show an entry without a stated response signature.

sequence.caption: Read the evidence the touch makes observable.
Two reads diverge from the same touch.
Each read yields a different decision.

annotations.text: Effort without upward progression: absorption, then rejection.
Lift through, retest holds, sell delta shrinks: acceptance.

fixture.description: Two synthetic touch sequences at 5484.00: (A) +1800 cumulative delta with five offer refreshes and no print above 5484.00, then flush to 5482.50; (B) similar delta but lift to 5484.75, pullback holds 5484.00 from above with shrinking sell delta.
fixture.notes: Both sequences must resolve within a three-minute window to match the chapter's claim that every distinguishing fact was visible in three minutes.

acceptance_criteria: All five required evidence items are drawn and visible.
Both reads (rejection and acceptance) branch from the same single touch event.
Each read terminates in a distinct decision state.
The figure never depicts the bare touch as an entry trigger.
Reveal sequence advances event -> evidence -> read -> decision in order.
